class.name: BlipKitTrack
class.description:
This class generates a single waveform and plays a [member note]. Method calls and property changes are thread-safe. [b]Note:[/b] When a [BlipKitTrack] is freed, it is automatically detached from [AudioStreamBlipKit] and all dividers are removed. [b]Example:[/b] Create a [BlipKitTrack] and attach it to an [AudioStreamBlipKit]: [codeblocks] [gdscript] # Create a track with the default waveform [BlipKitTrack.WAVEFORM_SQUARE]. # Ensure it is playing or has `autoplay` enabled. var _track := BlipKitTrack.new()
# An audio stream player with an [AudioStreamBlipKit] resource and `autoplay` enabled. @onready var stream_player: AudioStreamPlayer = $AudioStreamPlayer
func _ready() -> void: # Get the audio stream. var stream: AudioStreamBlipKit = stream_player.stream
# Attach the track to the audio stream. _track.attach(stream) # Play a note. _track.note = BlipKitTrack.NOTE_A_3 [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

method.name: add_divider
method.description: Adds a divider which calls [param callback] every multiple number of [i]ticks[/i] given by [param tick_interval]. For callbacks to be called, [BlipKitTrack] has to be attached to an [AudioStreamBlipKit] (see [method attach]). Callbacks are called in the same order as they are added. [param callback] does not receive any arguments and should return an [int] indicating whether to change the tick interval. If [param callback] returns [code]0[/code], the tick interval is not changed and [param callback] is called again after the same number of ticks. If [param callback] returns a value greater than [code]0[/code], the tick interval is permanently changed and [param callback] is called next after the returned number of ticks. If [param callback] returns a value less than [code]0[/code], the divider is removed. Returns an ID which can be used for [method remove_divider] or [method reset_divider]. [b]Note:[/b] Callbacks are called from the audio thread and should run as fast as possible to prevent distorted audio. Consider using [method Object.call_deferred] for functions which are expensive to run or should run on the main thread. [b]Note:[/b] [param callback] is called for the first time on the next tick. [b]Example:[/b] Add a divider and play a higher note on each call: [codeblocks] [gdscript] track.add_divider(60, func () -> int: track.note += 1.0 # Keep tick interval. return 0 ) [/gdscript] [/codeblocks] [b]Example:[/b] Add a divider and change the tick interval to [code]90[/code] after the first call: [codeblocks] [gdscript] track.add_divider(180, func () -> int: track.note += 1.0 # Change tick interval to 90. return 90 ) [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

method.name: attach
method.description: Attaches the track to an [AudioStreamBlipKit] and resumes all dividers from their last state.

method.name: create_with_waveform
method.description: Creates a [BlipKitTrack] with the given [param waveform] and sets [member master_volume] according to [enum Waveform].

method.name: detach
method.description: Detaches the track from its [AudioStreamBlipKit] and pauses all dividers.

method.name: get_dividers
method.description: Returns a list of divider IDs.

method.name: get_tremolo
method.description: Returns the tremolo values as [Dictionary]. Contains the keys [code]ticks[/code], [code]delta[/code], and [code]slide_ticks[/code]. [codeblock lang=gdscript] { ticks = 0, delta = 0.0, slide_ticks = 0 } [/codeblock]

method.name: get_vibrato
method.description: Returns the vibrato values as [Dictionary]. Contains the keys [code]ticks[/code], [code]delta[/code], and [code]slide_ticks[/code]. [codeblock lang=gdscript] { ticks = 0, delta = 0.0, slide_ticks = 0 } [/codeblock]

method.name: has_divider
method.description: Checks if a divider with [param id] exists.

method.name: mute
method.description: Mutes [member note] immediately without playing the release part of [member instrument] envelopes. Has the same effect as setting [member note] to [constant NOTE_MUTE]. This sets [member note] to [constant NOTE_RELEASE].

method.name: release
method.description: Releases [member note]. When [member instrument] is set, plays the release part of the [member instrument] envelopes. Has the same effect as setting [member note] to [constant NOTE_RELEASE].

method.name: reset
method.description: Resets the track properties to the initial values (except [member waveform] and [member master_volume]) and mutes [member note].

method.name: reset_divider
method.description: Resets the counter of a divider with [param id]. If [param tick_interval] is greater than [code]0[/code], the tick interval is changed to that value. [b]Note:[/b] The divider callback is called the first time on the next tick.

method.name: set_tremolo
method.description: Enables the tremolo effect. This decreases [member volume] periodically by [param delta] within the given number of [param ticks]. If [param slide_ticks] is greater than [code]0[/code], the current [code]ticks[/code] and [code]delta[/code] values are interpolated to the values within the given number of [i]ticks[/i] . If [param ticks] is [code]0[/code], the effect is disabled.

method.name: set_vibrato
method.description: Enables the vibrato effect. This increases and decreases [member note] periodically by [param delta] within the given number of [param ticks]. If [param slide_ticks] is greater than [code]0[/code], the current [code]ticks[/code] and [code]delta[/code] values are interpolated to the values within the given number of [i]ticks[/i] . If [param ticks] is [code]0[/code], the effect is disabled.

members.member: Sets the arpeggio sequence consisting of an array of pitch changes relative to the currently playing [member note]. The array can have a maximum size of [code]8[/code] and is truncated if it is larger. The duration for which each arpeggio note is defined with [member arpeggio_divider]. [b]Example:[/b] Play a major chord: [codeblocks] [gdscript] track.arpeggio = [0.0, 4.0, 7.0] [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]
Sets the number of [i]ticks[/i] each [member arpeggio] note is played.

Sets a custom waveform. If set, [member waveform] returns [constant WAVEFORM_CUSTOM]. If set to [code]null[/code] [member waveform] is reset to [constant WAVEFORM_SQUARE]. Setting a custom waveform mutes [member note]. [b]Note:[/b] Does not change [member master_volume].

Sets the duty cycle when [member waveform] is set to [constant WAVEFORM_SQUARE]. This defines the number of high amplitudes and is a value between [code]1[/code] and [code]15[/code]. Has no effect on other waveforms.
Sets the number of [i]ticks[/i] in which effects change the value of the corresponding property.
Sets the instrument when playing a note.
Sets the number of [i]ticks[/i] each instrument envelope value is played when no steps are defined.
Sets the mix volume. This is multiplied with [member volume] to be used as the output volume. [b]Note:[/b] This is also changed when setting the waveform (see [enum Waveform] for the default values).

Sets the note to play between [code]0.0[/code] (note C on octave [code]0[/code]) and [code]96.0[/code] (note C on octave [code]8[/code]) (see [enum Note]). Setting [constant NOTE_RELEASE] releases the note (see also [method release]). Setting [constant NOTE_MUTE] mutes the note (see also [method mute]). If [member instrument] is set, setting a note plays the attack and sustain part of the envelopes, whereas releasing the note plays the release part of the envelopes.
Sets the stereo panning between [code]-1.0[/code] and [code]+1.0[/code]. Negative values pan to the left channel and positive values to the right channel.
Sets the number of [i]ticks[/i] in which [member panning] changes to a new value.

Sets the number of wave phases after which the waveform is reset to the beginning. This has only an effect on [constant WAVEFORM_NOISE] or when [member custom_waveform] is set. If [param phase_wrap] is [code]0[/code], this behaviour is disabled. [b]Example:[/b] Limit noise to the first 32 phases: [codeblocks] [gdscript] var track := BlipKitTrack.create_with_waveform(BlipKitTrack.WAVEFORM_NOISE) track.phase_wrap = 32 [/gdscript] [/codeblocks] [b]Note:[/b] Non-power-of-two values change the pitch.
Sets the pitch values which is added to [member note]. [b]Example:[/b] Play all notes one octave lower: [codeblocks] [gdscript] track.pitch = -12.0 [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

Sets the number of [i]ticks[/i] in which [member note] changes to a new value. If no note is set at the moment, the note is played immediately. If set to [code]0[/code] when the effect is active sets [member note] to it's target value. [b]Example:[/b] Set portamento effect: [codeblocks] [gdscript] # Set portamento. track.portamento = 24 # Change note to C4 within 24 ticks from the previously set note. track.note = BlipKitTrack.NOTE_G_4 [/gdscript] [/codeblocks] [b]Example:[/b] Start portamento effect immediately: [codeblocks] [gdscript] # Set portamento. track.portamento = 24 # Assuming track.note is not (BlipKitTrack.NOTE_RELEASE) # Change note from C4 to G4 within 24 ticks. track.note = BlipKitTrack.NOTE_C_4 track.note = BlipKitTrack.NOTE_G_4 [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

Sets the sample to play. If set, [member waveform] returns [constant WAVEFORM_SAMPLE]. If set to [code]null[/code] [member waveform] is reset to [constant WAVEFORM_SQUARE]. Setting [member note] to [constant NOTE_C_4] plays the sample in it's original speed. Setting a sample mutes [member note]. [b]Example:[/b] Play a sample: [codeblocks] [gdscript] # Load sample from WAV resource. var wav := preload("res://example.wav") var sample := BlipKitSample.create_with_wav(wav) # Play the sample with it's original speed. track.sample = sample track.note = BlipKitTrack.NOTE_C_4 [/gdscript] [/codeblocks] [b]Note:[/b] Does not change [member master_volume].
Sets the pitch of the sample additionally to [member pitch].
Sets the note volume. This is multiplied with [member master_volume] to be used as the output volume.
Sets the number of [i]ticks[/i] in which [member volume] changes to a new value.

Sets the waveform. Also sets [member master_volume] accordingly (see [enum Waveform]) if [member master_volume] is not set yet. Setting a waveform mutes [member note]. The default is [constant WAVEFORM_SQUARE].

constants.constant: A square wave with 16 phases. The duty cycle (number of high amplitudes) can be set with [member duty_cycle]. Has a default [member master_volume] of [code]0.15[/code].
A triangle wave with 32 phases. Has a default [member master_volume] of [code]0.3[/code].
Generates noise using a 16-bit random generator. Looks like a square wave but with random low or high amplitudes. Has a default [member master_volume] of [code]0.15[/code].
A sawtooth wave with 7 phases. Has a default [member master_volume] of [code]0.15[/code].
A sine wave with 32 phases. Has a default [member master_volume] of [code]0.3[/code].
Set when a [member custom_waveform] is set. Cannot be set directly. Has a default [member master_volume] of [code]0.15[/code].
Set when [member sample] is set. Cannot be set directly. Has a default [member master_volume] of [code]0.3[/code].
Releases [member note]. Has the same effect as calling [method release].
Mutes [member note] immediately. Has the same effect as calling [method mute]. Sets [member note] to [constant NOTE_RELEASE].
